gene editing.news: UCB has completed the acquisition of Neurona Therapeutics, a company focused on regenerative science and cell therapies for epilepsy. The deal, valued at up to $1.15 billion, includes Neurona's lead asset rezanecel, which is currently under Phase I/II investigation for drug-resistant mesial temporal lobe epilepsy.

OXB announced that Cure Lowe Foundation has been selected for its VectorXcellerator Programme, a fully funded initiative supporting early-stage gene therapy development. The selection covers lentiviral vector and AAV programmes, providing feasibility-level data to guide next development steps without obligation for future commercial engagement.

bispecific antibodies.news: Immunocore presented updated Phase 1 data for brenetafusp, a PRAME-targeted ImmTAC bispecific antibody, showing an 87% 6-month overall survival rate in heavily pretreated advanced melanoma. The data supports the selection of a 160 mcg dose for the ongoing Phase 3 PRISM-MEL-301 trial evaluating brenetafusp in combination with nivolumab.

gene editing.news: Tune Therapeutics presented positive Phase 1b/2a proof-of-concept data for TUNE-401, an epigenetic silencer utilizing its TEMPO platform to directly silence HBV cccDNA. The study demonstrated durable antiviral activity and biomarker loss in patients with chronic hepatitis B, with plans to advance the candidate into a larger Phase 2 trial in late 2026.

monoclonal antibodies.news: GSK announced positive pivotal data for bepirovirsen, an investigational antisense oligonucleotide (ASO), achieving a 19% functional cure rate in chronic hepatitis B patients in the B-Well 1 and B-Well 2 Phase III trials. The results, published in the New England Journal of Medicine, showed a statistically significant improvement over placebo and support the drug's potential to redefine treatment for the disease.

The FDA approved Imfinzi (durvalumab) in combination with BCG for the treatment of BCG-naïve, high-risk non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer (NMIBC). This approval, based on the Phase III POTOMAC trial, marks the first new therapy in over 30 years for this setting, showing a 32% reduction in the risk of recurrence, progression, or death.

The CARES Phase III clinical programme for anselamimab, a monoclonal antibody targeting amyloid fibrils, failed to meet its primary endpoint in the overall light chain amyloidosis population. However, a prespecified subgroup analysis of patients with kappa light chain amyloidosis demonstrated a 62% improvement in survival and a 71% reduction in cardiovascular hospitalizations.

The U.S. FDA approved DECNUPAZ (pivekimab sunirine-pvzy), a CD123-targeting antibody-drug conjugate (ADC), for the treatment of adult patients with blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN). This is the first and only ADC approved for this ultra-rare blood cancer and marks AbbVie's first ADC approval for a blood cancer indication.

Pfizer and Innovent Biologics entered a global strategic collaboration to research and develop 12 early-stage and de novo cancer medicines. The portfolio includes antibody-drug conjugates (ADCs) and multi-specific antibodies, with Pfizer receiving exclusive global or outside-Greater China licenses for eight programs and co-developing four programs globally.

The National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) of China accepted new drug applications (NDAs) for toripalimab injection (subcutaneous) across 12 tumor indications. JS001sc is the first domestic anti-PD-1 monoclonal antibody in a subcutaneous formulation to enter the marketing application stage, offering a more convenient administration method compared to intravenous options.

gene editing.news: Kyowa Kirin and Kura Oncology initiated a Japanese Phase 2 registration-directed trial for ziftomenib, an oral menin inhibitor, in patients with relapsed or refractory NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia. The trial aims to support a regulatory filing in Japan following completion, building on the drug's prior FDA approval in the US.

bispecific antibodies.news: Genentech will present extensive data at ARVO 2026 showcasing Vabysmo, the first bispecific antibody approved for the eye, with new real-world data confirming its potent retinal drying capabilities in wet AMD and DME.

Pfizer announced positive interim results from the Phase 3 MagnetisMM-5 trial for ELREXFIO (elranatamab), a BCMA-CD3 directed bispecific antibody, demonstrating a statistically significant improvement in progression-free survival for patients with relapsed or refractory multiple myeloma.

Roche confirmed it will present data at ARVO 2026 highlighting Vabysmo, a bispecific antibody targeting Ang-2 and VEGF-A, which showed improved retinal anatomy in real-world clinical practice for nAMD and DME patients.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Genentech announced early data from the Phase 2 ALLUVIUM and BARDENAS trials for vamikibart, an investigational monoclonal antibody targeting IL-6, showing efficacy in treating diabetic macular edema.

Roche announced it will present early results for vamikibart, an investigational intravitreal monoclonal antibody targeting IL-6, at ARVO 2026, highlighting its potential in treating uveitic macular edema and diabetic macular edema.

gene editing.news: Gilead Sciences completed the acquisition of Arcellx for approximately $7.8 billion to gain full control of anitocabtagene autoleucel (anito-cel), a BCMA-directed CAR T-cell therapy for multiple myeloma. The acquisition consolidates ownership to accelerate development and streamline decision-making ahead of the potential commercial launch of the cell therapy.

Affinia Therapeutics announced it will present new preclinical data on AFTX-201, an investigational AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy, at the ASGCT 2026 Annual Meeting. The company highlighted that the FDA has accepted its IND application and granted Fast Track designation, while the EMA has granted Orphan Drug designation for the program.

Astellas announced a strategic halt of its gene therapy candidate AT132 (resamirigene bilparvovec) for X-linked myotubular myopathy and recorded a ¥16.4 billion impairment loss. The company is redeploying resources to ASP2957, a next-generation investigational gene therapy.

The European Medicines Agency's Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) adopted a positive opinion recommending marketing authorization for Itvisma (intrathecal onasemnogene abeparvovec), a gene replacement therapy for spinal muscular atrophy (SMA) in patients aged two years and older.

bispecific antibodies.news: WuXi Biologics received GMP certification from the South Korea Ministry of Food and Drug Safety (MFDS) for three facilities, enabling commercial manufacturing of a bispecific antibody therapeutic for biliary tract cancer.

gene editing.news: Roche announced the launch of an NVIDIA AI factory to accelerate the development of new therapeutics and diagnostics, leveraging advanced computational infrastructure to support its pipeline which includes gene-editing and other novel modalities.

Insilico Medicine nominated ISM0387, its first UAE-based preclinical candidate and 30th AI-supported preclinical candidate overall, utilizing generative AI to design a novel PRMT5 inhibitor for glioblastoma.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Genentech announced positive Phase III METEOROID study results showing that Enspryng (satralizumab), an IL-6 receptor monoclonal antibody, reduced the risk of relapse by 68% in patients with myelin oligodendrocyte glycoprotein antibody-associated disease (MOGAD). The data, presented at the 2026 American Academy of Neurology Annual Meeting, supports a regulatory submission to potentially secure the first approved treatment for this rare disease.

Disc Medicine announced that data from the RALLY-MF Phase 2 trial of DISC-0974, an investigational anti-hemojuvelin monoclonal antibody, will be presented as an oral abstract at the 2026 ASCO Annual Meeting. The study evaluates the agent's efficacy in treating anemia associated with myelofibrosis.

The FDA accepted a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for Gazyva (obinutuzumab) for the treatment of systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) based on Phase III ALLEGORY trial data showing significant disease activity reduction. A decision on approval is expected by December 2026.

The FDA granted Priority Review to a supplemental Biologics License Application (sBLA) for PADCEV (enfortumab vedotin) in combination with Keytruda (pembrolizumab) for perioperative treatment of muscle-invasive bladder cancer regardless of cisplatin eligibility. The PDUFA target date is August 17, 2026.

AstraZeneca's Ultomiris (ravulizumab) met its primary endpoint in the Phase III I CAN trial, demonstrating a statistically significant and clinically meaningful reduction in proteinuria in adults with immunoglobulin A nephropathy (IgAN) at week 34. The company plans to seek accelerated approval in key markets.

gene editing.news: Gilead Sciences received all required regulatory approvals for its acquisition of Arcellx, a company developing anitocabtagene autoleucel (anito-cel), a gene-edited CAR-T cell therapy. The tender offer for Arcellx shares has been extended to April 27, 2026, with a purchase price of $115.00 per share plus a contingent value right.

UCB announced a definitive agreement to acquire Neurona Therapeutics for an upfront payment of $650 million and up to $500 million in milestones. The deal includes NRTX-1001, a regenerative pluripotent stem cell therapy currently in Phase I/II trials for drug-resistant mesial temporal lobe epilepsy, which utilizes cell engineering to restore neural circuitry.

Astellas announced the first patient has been dosed in a Phase 3 registrational study of setidegrasib (ASP3082), a novel KRAS G12D-targeted protein degrader, for the treatment of metastatic pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma. This marks the first protein degrader targeting a KRAS mutation to enter Phase 3 development.

STORM Therapeutics secured $56 million in Series C financing and announced the dosing of the first patient in a Phase 2 monotherapy trial of STC-15, a first-in-class METTL3 inhibitor, for select sarcoma indications. The funding will support the advancement of this RNA epigenetics therapy.

bispecific antibodies.news: Hinge Bio announced the first subject dosed in a Phase 1 clinical trial of HB2198, a novel multispecific antibody (GEM-DIMER) targeting both CD19 and CD20 with enhanced dual Fc domains for treating autoimmune diseases like Systemic Lupus Erythematosus. A separate Phase 1 trial in SLE patients is also open to screening.

gene editing.news: OXB launched a fast-track offering for AAV and lentiviral vector platforms to accelerate viral vector development and manufacture. The new service aims to reduce the timeline to GMP manufacturing by up to nine months for biotechs utilizing these vectors.

Cartherics and Catalent expanded their commercial license agreement to utilize a Catalent cGMP-compliant induced pluripotent stem cell (iPSC) line for the manufacture of CAR-NK cell therapies. The partnership supports the development of CTH-401, an iPSC-derived CAR-NK product, with FDA approval already granted for the use of the licensed iPSC line as the starting cell material.

Généthon scientists published a study in Molecular Therapy detailing a CRISPR/Cas9 strategy to increase utrophin expression in Duchenne Muscular Dystrophy (DMD). By using gene editing to deactivate a Let7c microRNA binding site, the team successfully boosted utrophin levels in human DMD cells and murine models, offering a potential universal therapeutic approach independent of specific DMD mutations.

monoclonal antibodies.news: China's National Medical Products Administration (NMPA) approved Exdensur (depemokimab), an ultra-long-acting monoclonal antibody, for the treatment of chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP) in adults. The approval is based on data from the ANCHOR-1 and ANCHOR-2 phase III trials showing significant improvements in nasal polyp size and obstruction.

Gilead Sciences entered into a definitive agreement to acquire Tubulis GmbH for $3.15 billion in upfront cash, plus up to $1.85 billion in milestone payments. The acquisition adds Tubulis' next-generation antibody-drug conjugate (ADC) platform and clinical assets, including TUB-040, to Gilead's oncology pipeline.

Chugai Pharmaceutical and Zenyaku Kogyo announced the termination of their co-promotion agreement for Rituxan (rituximab), an anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody, in Japan. Effective September 3, 2026, Zenyaku Kogyo will assume sole responsibility for sales and medical information for the product.

gene editing.news: CRISPR Therapeutics announced that its senior management team will present at the 25th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference on April 13, 2026. The company highlighted its leadership in gene editing, including the approved CASGEVY therapy and its proprietary SyNTase editing platform.

bispecific antibodies.news: Sanofi announced that its bispecific Nanobody lunsekimig met primary and key secondary endpoints in Phase 2 studies for moderate-to-severe asthma (AIRCULES) and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (DUET). However, the drug failed to meet its primary endpoint in an exploratory Phase 2 study for atopic dermatitis (VELVET).

Immunocore announced that its management team will present at the 25th Annual Needham Virtual Healthcare Conference to discuss its pipeline of TCR bispecific immunotherapies (ImmTAX), including its approved product KIMMTRAK for uveal melanoma.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Sanofi announced that lunsekimig, a novel bispecific Nanobody (VHH) targeting TSLP and IL-13, met primary and key secondary endpoints in Phase 2 studies for asthma and chronic rhinosinusitis with nasal polyps (CRSwNP), though it failed to meet the primary endpoint in atopic dermatitis.

Amgen reported positive topline results from a Phase 3 trial for subcutaneous TEPEZZA (teprotumumab), a monoclonal antibody for Thyroid Eye Disease, which met its primary endpoint with a 77% proptosis response rate and demonstrated efficacy comparable to the intravenous formulation.

gene editing.news: Affinia Therapeutics received approval from Health Canada to initiate the UPBEAT trial, a Phase 1/2 clinical study investigating AFTX-201, an AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy. This follows FDA acceptance of the IND and Fast Track designation for the investigational medicine.

Affinia Therapeutics received approval from Health Canada to initiate the UPBEAT trial, a Phase 1/2 clinical study investigating AFTX-201, an AAV gene therapy for BAG3-associated dilated cardiomyopathy. This follows FDA acceptance of the IND application and Fast Track designation for the investigational medicine.

monoclonal antibodies.news: Lipocine reported topline safety and efficacy results for LPCN 1154 (oral brexanolone) in a Phase 3 trial for postpartum depression. While the primary endpoint was not met in the overall population, a post hoc analysis in patients with a history of psychiatric conditions showed statistically significant and clinically meaningful reductions in HAM-D scores. The drug demonstrated a favorable safety profile with no severe adverse events.

gene editing.news: Caris Life Sciences launched Caris ChromoSeq, the world's first Whole Genome and Whole Transcriptome Tumor Profiling assay specifically designed for myeloid malignancies. The test aims to replace fragmented diagnostic workflows with a single, integrated solution for acute myeloid leukemia, myelodysplastic syndromes, and myeloproliferative neoplasms.

bispecific antibodies.news: Positive results from the EMERALD-3 Phase III trial showed that Imfinzi (durvalumab) plus Imjudo (tremelimumab), combined with lenvatinib and TACE, significantly improved progression-free survival in patients with unresectable hepatocellular carcinoma eligible for embolization.
